channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lee Andrews comments on marital rumours and shares personal updates on social media
item.description: Lee Andrews, husband of glamour model Katie Price, has publicly addressed speculation regarding tensions in their marriage via a series of social media posts. Andrews, 42, has faced criticism and alle...

item.title: Abbott shares drop after weak 2026 outlook and slowdown in nutrition, diabetes and diagnostics units
item.description: Abbott Laboratories shares fell roughly 6% after the company released its first‑quarter results and 2026 outlook, which disappointed many analysts on Wall Street. The medical‑device and nutrition gian...

item.title: EFTA Bond Raises Sh33 Billion to Support SMEs in Tanzania
item.description: Equity for Tanzania Limited (EFTA) has successfully raised Sh33.04 billion from its corporate bond, well above its initial target of Sh15 billion, achieving a 220 percent subscription rate. The bond w...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital successfully conducts laparoscopic procedure on patient in Dodoma
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital (BMH) in Dodoma has achieved another milestone in Tanzania’s health sector by successfully conducting a complex laparoscopic surgery on a patient from Mvumi Village in Manyoni ...

item.title: Eastern Cape Premier Mabuyane sues Malema for defamation over degree allegations
item.description: Eastern Cape Premier and ANC provincial chairperson Oscar Mabuyane has formally opened a defamation case against Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader Julius Malema. This follows Malema’s public clai...

item.title: Former Arsenal Goalkeeper Alex Manninger Dies at 48 in Salzburg Train Accident
item.description: Former Arsenal goalkeeper Alexander Manninger don die tragically on Thursday, April 16, 2026, after him car collide with train for Salzburg region for Austria. The 48-year-old ex-keeper, wey play for ...

item.title: Oil futures rise again as U.S. ramps up blockade in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil futures extended their gains Thursday as the United States intensified its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime passage for global oil shipments. The move has tightened supply availabi...

item.title: Tanzania to Host Miss World 2027 Pageant
item.description: Vipi! Tanzania imefanikiwa kupalinda nafasi ya kuwa mwenyeji wa mashindano ya Miss World mwaka 2027. Hii imetangazwa na Waziri wa Habari, Utamaduni, Sanaa na Michezo, Bwana Paul Makonda, wakati akizun...

item.title: Bluesky Social Network Hit by Denial-of-Service Attack, Experiencing Service Issues
item.description: The popular decentralized social network, Bluesky, is currently experiencing significant service disruptions following a denial-of-service (DoS) attack. The issues began around 2:42 a.m. ET on Thursda...

item.title: Mabuyane Takes Legal Action Against Malema Over Alleged Degree Misrepresentation
item.description: Eastern Cape premier and ANC provincial chair Oscar Mabuyane has formally opened a defamation case against EFF leader Julius Malema following Malema's public allegations that Mabuyane fraudulently obt...

item.title: Police Medical Scheme Data Breach Puts South African Officers at Risk
item.description: A significant data breach has placed South African police officers and their families at risk, following a cyberattack on the South African Police Service Medical Scheme. The hacker group, calling its...

item.title: Legacy in Bloom celebrates Filipino fashion, craftsmanship, and community at Makati Garden Club
item.description: The Makati Garden Club recently held 'Legacy in Bloom,' an event that brought together Filipino designers, artists, and advocates to celebrate local creativity and purposeful living. Hosted at the ser...

item.title: President Samia urges upkeep of cancer diagnostic equipment at ORCI
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has instructed the management of the Ocean Road Cancer Institute (ORCI) to make sure that modern diagnostic machines, including the PET-CT Scan, are properly maintained s...

item.title: Trump Dismisses Need for Meeting With the Pope Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump said he did not think a meeting with Pope Leo XIV was necessary, continuing a public back-and-forth tied to the war with Iran and the administration’s hard line on nuclear issue...

item.title: NBI Launches Probe into Davao Mayor Baste Duterte's Remark on 'One Head to Roll' Amid Alleged Assassination Plot Claims
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has started looking into a controversial statement made by Davao City Mayor Sebastian “Baste” Duterte during the launch of the Reform Alliance for Good Gover...

item.title: Nigeria's Intelligence Crisis: Unverified Reports Impact Airlines and International Relations
item.description: Wahala dey for Nigeria! Things bin gett serious as Nigerian security agencies dem dey battle wit a parallel intelligence network, dem say e dey operate for secret. Information dey leak to foreign miss...

item.title: Conservatives oppose Liberal plans to reshape parliamentary committees after majority win
item.description: Conservative House Leader Andrew Scheer says the new Liberal majority should not be used to change the composition of parliamentary committees in a way that sidelines opposition MPs. He argued that co...

item.title: Mwinyi highlights women’s strong performance in leadership and outlines empowerment initiatives in Zanzibar
item.description: Zanzibar President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi has said that women in Zanzibar and Tanzania at large are performing very well when given opportunities in leadership and decision-making positions. His remark...

item.title: Basata Confirms 361 Degrees Africa as Official Miss World Tanzania Organizer
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania’s National Arts Council, Basata, has officially clarified the situation in the beauty pageant industry, ending all confusion over who can send a contestant to Miss World. The ...

item.title: Ford Rejects Grocery Pricing Surveillance Ban, Vows Action Against Collusion
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has stated that his government will not be implementing a ban on so-called surveillance pricing for groceries, a measure recently proposed by Manitoba. Ford maintains his bel...
